---
title: "TimeBack Alpha Read Packager â€” agent reference"
format: gfm
---

# Agent reference â€” Alpha Read Packager (`arpack`)

Drive `arpack.py` to assemble a **contract-valid Alpha Read course** (OneRoster v1p2 + QTI 3.0)
from QTI items + a skeleton â€” or `course_orchestrator.py` to build a whole course in one command. The
contract is reverse-engineered from a live production course export (1077 items) **and** cross-checked against
Ilma's `incept-timeback-plugin` skill (where we differed, she won â€” corrections applied below).

## The model (verified, do not deviate)
`Course (alpha_read markers + publish flags) â†’ flat units â†’ resource per lesson (lessonType:"alpha-read-article") â†’ QTI assessment-test â†’ items + stimulus`.
- Per lesson: **3â€“6 guiding** items (1 passage + 1 MCQ each, carries a stimulus-ref) **+ exactly 4 quiz** items (no stimulus).
- Items: **all 7 r2 formats** handled end-to-end via Ilma's RULE 1 split â€” JSON-safe (`choice`/`order`/`extended-text`/single-blank `text-entry`) emit JSON; the rest (`hot-text`/`match`/`ebsr`/â€¦ + multi-blank `text-entry`) ride as raw `<qti-assessment-item>` XML verbatim. Answer key from `qti-correct-response`.
- Every item carries its **question stem** in `interaction.questionStructure.prompt` (Ilma's JSON template shape, create-mcq.md); options nest in `questionStructure.choices`. A stemless item renders as naked options â€” the validator fail-closes on it.
- **App ownership (Ramish breaking change, â‰Ą2026-06-30):** root `course.primaryApp` is the ONLY ownership setter and must be exactly `"alpha_read"`; `metadata.primaryApp` is now **inert** (mirrored for pre-cutover servers, forward-safe to drop).
- Passage HTML âŠ† `div/p/h1/h2/strong/em/br/blockquote` + MathML; **images must be S3 URLs** (no other media). Standards do **not** serialise (live course tags none).

## Run it
1. **Ingest the generator output (no reshaping):** `output_dir_ingester.from_timeback_build_output(dir)` reads its `manifest.json` + `items/*.xml` + `stimuli/*.xml`. Or one item: `arpack.from_qti_xml(xml)` â†’ `{identifier,title,role,stimulus_ref,prompt,choices,correct_id(s)}` (role auto-inferred; `adapt_qti_item` **raises** if the key isn't among choices).
2. **Ingest the skeleton:** `skeleton_adapter.from_skeleton_table("expeditions.csv")` â†’ units (bandâ†’Lexile, genre, coverage).
3. **Assemble â†’ validate â†’ emit:** `python3 src/arpack.py build skeleton.json out/` (fail-closed validator runs first). Or **one command**: `python3 src/course_orchestrator.py skeleton.json out/`.

## Validator rejects (fail-closed)
non-S3 media Â· MCQ <2 choices Â· key not among choices Â· **missing question stem** (`interaction.questionStructure.prompt`) Â·
missing markers/publish flags Â· **root `primaryApp` not exactly `"alpha_read"`** (null/empty/non-string/aliasâ†’422) Â·
authored `metadata.metrics` Â· id-coupling breaks Â· wrong section structure Â· content/prompt not well-formed XHTML after sanitize.

## Item checklist â€” what's enforced where
**[pack]** packager `validate()`, fail-closed Â· **[gen]** generation / the jsonlâ†’QTI transform (the packager can't see it; InceptBench doesn't check it either) Â· **[probe]** live render in AlphaRead, unconfirmed until the test-student probe.

- **Every item:** [pack] one of the 7 types with correct RULE 1 routing; well-formed XML; stem present; root id matches the ref; every `<img>` is an https **S3** URL (no relative paths, no other media). [gen] near-neighbour distractors; Grade-3 Lexile.
- **MCQ/MSQ (`choice`):** [pack] key âŠ† options; exactly 1 key (MCQ) / â‰Ą1 (MSQ); â‰Ą2 options.
- **Drag-to-order (`order`):** [pack] cardinality `ordered`; key ranks every option.
- **Fill-in (`text-entry`):** [pack] â‰Ą1 non-empty accepted answer; single-blank â†’ JSON, multi-blank â†’ XML with **per-blank** keys (blank N never accepts blank M's answer).
- **Hot-text / match / EBSR (XML-only):** [pack] checks **only** that the envelope is well-formed QTI, has a matching identifier, and is S3-clean â€” it does **not** inspect the answer key or options (RULE 1). Content correctness is therefore [gen]: hot-text's correct token IS the evidence span; match pairs are complete + unambiguous; **EBSR Part B options are verbatim from the passage and depend on Part A**. [probe] all three: does AlphaRead render them?
- **`write_prompt` (Day-2 Read & Write):** 1 MCQ (name the text structure) + 1 hot-text (click the proof) â€” no typing. Both rows above apply; the hot-text [probe] is the load-bearing unknown.
- **Course:** [pack] root `primaryApp:"alpha_read"`; publish flags; 3â€“6 guiding + exactly 4 quiz per lesson; resourceâ†”test id-coupling; no duplicate ids; `lessonType:"alpha-read-article"`.

The headline: the packager guarantees items are **well-formed and shippable**; for hot-text/match/EBSR it does **not** vouch for content correctness â€” that's on generation, and IB doesn't check it.

## Render (live behavior â€” what AlphaRead shows)
Vertical articleâ†’questionâ†’unlock. Per lesson: passage + MCQs. The QTI API accepts the tech-enhanced formats,
but **whether AlphaRead renders them is unverified** â€” the open
**renderer probe** (needs a test-student account, not real children). Keep MCQ/MSQ as the safe default.

## Cross-check vs Ilma's skill â€” corrections APPLIED
- **D3** component-resource links carry `status:"active"` + `metadata.lessonType` (else 422).
- **G2** every test + item carries `qti-outcome-declaration[SCORE]` (else score always 0).
- **G1** all stimulus/choice/**prompt** content runs through Ilma's `sanitize_html.full_sanitize` (XHTML); `validate()` gates on well-formedness. *Highest-value â€” the #1 render bug.*
- **G3** the question **stem** is emitted into `interaction.questionStructure.prompt` (+ options under `questionStructure.choices`) â€” Ilma's JSON template (create-mcq.md / create-frq.md). Previously the parsed prompt was dropped on emit â†’ items rendered as naked choices. Now fail-closed.
- **A2** root `primaryApp` is the validator's **hard gate** (exact `"alpha_read"`; null/empty/non-string/alias â†’ 422); `metadata.primaryApp` relaxed to **inert/optional** (package valid if dropped; flagged only if present-and-wrong).
- **D4** item-ref `href` = bare `{id}.xml`; **D5** MCQ choice floor relaxed to â‰Ą2; resource `url` â†’ https.
- **D1/D2 (Ilma RULE 1 â€” IMPLEMENTED):** JSON-safe is exactly `choice` / `extended-text` / `order` / single-blank `text-entry` â†’ emitted as JSON. `hottext` / `match` / `associate` / `inline-choice` / `gap-match` / `ebsr` / `select-point` **and multi-blank `text-entry`** â†’ emitted as the raw-XML envelope `{"format":"xml","xml":<rawXml>}`, carried byte-verbatim (the JSONâ†’XML converter corrupts their scoring otherwise). The invented `map_response` template is gone (Ilma uses `match_correct`). The split is decided **per item** (`_is_xml_only`); `validate()` checks XML-only items as a well-formed envelope, not as a JSON option model.

## Push â€” via Ilma's `/timeback` skill, NOT hand-rolled
`arpack` is a clean offline emitter. The upload is Ilma's surface (45+ nightly tests vs the live API): she
owns auth (Cognito body creds + token cache + 401 refresh), the push order (S3â†’courseâ†’unitsâ†’lessonsâ†’itemsâ†’
testsâ†’stimuliâ†’resources(trailing `/`)â†’links, 409=success, checkpoint each), JSON-vs-XML branching, and
GET-modify-PUT. Pre-flight: write-scoped Cognito client + `POST /validate`. Push only to a
`STAN-PROBE-DELETEMEâ€¦` draft; post-push, open â‰Ą1 lesson in the student UI (200 â‰  rendered).

## Verified vs open
- **Verified (offline):** ingests the real `incept-qti-sdk` output unedited (the 4-item + 13-item all-7 fixtures); parses all 1077 items of the live course export cleanly (no wrong keys/blanks); emits shapes that match that export field-for-field; one-command orchestrator â†’ package passes `validate`; sanitizer + Phase-1 corrections applied; cross-checked against Ilma's skill (her corrections adopted where we differed).
- **Open:** a real multi-lesson course end-to-end on the live skeleton + full generator output; live rendering of the tech-enhanced formats is unverified; **never POSTed to a server** (shape-match, not server-accept) â€” closed by `POST /validate` + the Ilma-pipeline push.
